*[Bug 19002] Referent of 'it' in R CMD check NOTE about LinkingTo is ambiguous*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19002>

Martin Maechler (<mailto:maechler@stat.math.ethz.ch|maechler@stat.math.ethz.ch>) changed:

           What    |Removed                     |Added
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
                 CC|                            |maechler@stat.math.ethz.ch

--- Comment #5 from Martin Maechler (<mailto:maechler@stat.math.ethz.ch|maechler@stat.math.ethz.ch>) ---
(In reply to Sebastian Meyer from comment #3)
I agree, and I think simpler is better (in a case where it is still fully
precise).
Can we adopt it (and close)?

text:
*[Bug 19009] New: tools::file_path_sans_ext() does not recognise file extension of names that end in a dot.*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19009>

            Bug ID: 19009
           Summary: tools::file_path_sans_ext() does not recognise file
                    extension of names that end in a dot.
           Product: R
           Version: R 4.5.x
          Hardware: Other
                OS: Windows 64-bit
            Status: UNCONFIRMED
          Severity: minor
          Priority: P5
         Component: Language
          Assignee: <mailto:R-core@R-project.org|R-core@R-project.org>
          Reporter: <mailto:jessealderliesten@hotmail.com|jessealderliesten@hotmail.com>

`tools::file_path_sans_ext()` does not recognise the extension of file names
that end in a dot, whereas `tools::file_ext()` does recognise such extensions:

`filename <- "ab..txt"`
`tools::file_path_sans_ext(filename)` # `"ab..txt"` instead of expected `"ab."`
`tools::file_ext(filename)` # returns `"txt"`, as expected.

This is caused by a difference in the used regular expressions: the regex
`"([^.]+)\\.[[:alnum:]]+$"` for `tools::file_path_sans_ext()` excludes file
names ending in a dot, whereas the regex `"\\.([[:alnum:]]+)$"` for
`tools::file_ext()` does not.

Therefore
`paste0(tools::file_path_sans_ext(filename), ".", tools::file_ext(filename))`
gives `"ab..txt.txt"`, whereas it should recreate `filename`: `"ab..txt"`.

The patch harmonises tools::file_path_sans_ext() with tools::file_ext() to
recognise the extension of file names that end in a dot.

The link to the patch file:
<https://github.com/r-devel/r-svn/pull/236/commits/0139e88cf64b622f2879937fd5aaf0871d1f0e45>

I do not know where to add regression tests, but a relevant one would be:
`filename <- "ab..txt"`
`stopifnot(filename == paste0(tools::file_path_sans_ext(filename), ".",
tools::file_ext(filename)))`

The difference between tools::file_path_sans_ext() and tools::file_ext() was
introduced before april 2013 (I cannot look further back):
<https://github.com/r-devel/r-svn/blame/b216bc9200bd37c473cfff2ed24fe4c218d0893a/src/library/tools/R/utils.R#L23>

text:
*[Bug 19010] New: Compilation fails with Intel OneAPI 2024.2 compiler*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19010>

            Bug ID: 19010
           Summary: Compilation fails with Intel OneAPI 2024.2 compiler
           Product: R
           Version: R-devel (trunk)
          Hardware: x86_64/x64/amd64 (64-bit)
                OS: Linux
            Status: UNCONFIRMED
          Severity: normal
          Priority: P5
         Component: Graphics
          Assignee: <mailto:R-core@R-project.org|R-core@R-project.org>
          Reporter: <mailto:csardi.gabor@gmail.com|csardi.gabor@gmail.com>

```
intel-1  | /opt/intel/oneapi/compiler/2024.2/bin/icx -std=gnu23
-I"../../../../include" -DNDEBUG -I../../../include -I../../../../src/include
-DHAVE_CONFIG_H -I../../../../src/main  -I/usr/local/include  
-fvisibility=hidden -fpic  -O3 -fp-model precise -Wall -Wstrict-prototypes  -c
plot3d.c -o plot3d.o
intel-1  | plot3d.c:807:17: error: use of undeclared identifier 'atan2pi'
intel-1  |   807 |         angle = 180. * atan2pi(dy, dx);
intel-1  |       |                        ^
intel-1  | 1 error generated.
```

This is Fedora 38 with the OneAPI packages:
